p.seg: Thurstan son of Living to all sons of Holy Mother Church, Greeting:

Be it known to you that I have granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of Holyrood at Maidens’ Castle and to the canons serving God there, the church of Livingston with half a ploughgate of land and one toft and with all its rights and things that belong to it, just as my father gave to them, in free and perpetual alms. I therefore will that the aforesaid canons should possess the aforenamed church as freely and quietly as any church in the whole of Lothian is freely and quietly possessed.

With these witnesses, Geoffrey abbot of Dunfermline, Amfred of Newbattle, Walter monk of that place, Geoffrey of Lasswade, Peter the chaplain of Loquhariot, Walter the earl’s chaplain, Simon of Ramsay, Roger the deacon, David son of Geoffrey, Martin the clerk, Walter the king’s chamberlain at Roxburgh, William de Aldri.
